questions.question: Impulse ratio of a rod gap -

explanations.a: Incorrect. Impulse ratio is not unity for rod gaps.
explanations.b: Correct. Impulse ratio (impulse breakdown voltage / peak power frequency breakdown voltage) for rod gaps is typically 1.2 to 1.5.
explanations.c: Incorrect. That range is too high.

questions.question: which electrode has minimum breakdown voltage -

explanations.a: Incorrect. Spheres produce uniform field, requiring higher voltage.
explanations.b: Correct. Pointed electrodes (sharp points) cause field concentration, leading to corona and breakdown at lower voltages.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Flat electrodes require higher voltage.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Large spheres give more uniform field, higher breakdown voltage.

questions.question: The insulation of the modern EHV lines is designed based on -

explanations.a: Incorrect. For EHV (400 kV and above), switching surges are more critical than lightning.
explanations.b: Correct. Modern EHV lines are designed based on switching overvoltages, which are higher than lightning surges at these voltage levels.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Corona affects losses and RI, not basic insulation level.

questions.question: Minimum sparking potential of Air is about -

explanations.b: Incorrect. That is for a specific gap distance.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Too low for Paschen's minimum.
explanations.d: Correct. Paschen's minimum for air is approximately 325 V at a pressure × distance (pd) of about 0.75 Torr·cm.

answers.b: Both DC and AC voltages upto high frequency
answers.d: Only DC and AC peak voltages

explanations.a: Incorrect. It can also measure AC.
explanations.b: Correct. Electrostatic voltmeters measure RMS of both DC and AC voltages accurately up to high frequencies.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Not suitable for fast impulses due to mechanical inertia.
explanations.d: Incorrect. It measures RMS, not peak.
